chemistry_overall: All candidates have dual-criterion mixing failures and are hard-gated. Concentration increases could improve mixing but won't resolve the fundamental Da_mass > 1.0 issue. All candidates suffer from dual-criterion mixing failures with high Da_mass values and poor mixing ratios. Candidates 3 and 6 additionally fail conversion requirements. All candidates fail dual-criterion mixing requirements with Da_mass > 1.0 AND r_mix > 0.20, triggering hard gates. Concentration reduction to 0.05 M could potentially resolve mixing issues. All candidates have hard-gate violations making them impractical for bench implementation.

safety_overall: All candidates show dual-criterion mixing failures with high Damköhler numbers and poor radial mixing. Candidates 1, 2, and 37 also have insufficient residence time for target conversion. All candidates show mixing limitations and insufficient conversion. All have hard gate failures for dual-criterion mixing and conversion below minimum thresholds. All candidates have dual-criterion mixing failures with high Damköhler numbers and poor radial mixing ratios. Most show inadequate conversion below 85% target. Multiple candidates have severe mixing limitations and impractical reactor lengths. High pressure drops and hard gate violations present significant safety concerns.
